title: The MACU Report — Episode 10 — Exact Change
notes: FULL REWRITE (but/therefore, comedy-forward). Unit 7 takes the moon as tribute; Ron destroys himself bargaining it back; oblivious Walter accidentally pays with a secret and the moon returns. Ron's breakdown is the spine; Walter oblivious; full reset. NO new clones (all reused). MAX CLIP VARIETY: nearly every line is its own unique master (consistent face via per-character seed, unique core/key) — ~44 unique masters, minimal reuse, on purpose. SILENT BEATS are HOLD-PLAY (hold_style:'play' — ANIMATE, do NOT first-frame): c14,c17,c27,c36,c44. New tonal SFX synthesized into assets/sfx/ (vend_powerdown/up, bulb_click, coins_reject); a few realistic ones to be acquired CC0 by Max (coins_insert, metal_clink, tooth_click, whistle_faint). CUT from prior version: Chaz/Spicoli, Vela/Sagan, Signal monologues, the bonus, the PLEASE STAND BY/OBSERVATORY cards.

comfyui.note: zeroscope_v2_576w (watermark-free). 384x384x24f locked - do not bump resolution.

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST {text: vo} to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): generate a silent wav of hold_seconds, no subtitle. hold_style 'freeze' = first-frame still (double-take); hold_style 'play' = PLAY THE CLIP (animate) - ep10's holds are ALL 'play'. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Apply jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx one-shots, burn subs -> final/ep10.mp4.

music.note: Intro + outro big-band beds only this episode; the bargaining plays dry for tension.

sfx.note: One-shots mixed at each cue's start via the stage-5 adelay->amix chain (VO stays full). vend_powerdown/vend_powerup/bulb_click/coins_reject were synthesized into assets/sfx/ already; cricket_chirp exists in the kit. ACQUIRE (Max, CC0 freesound, exact filenames): coins_insert.wav, metal_clink.wav, tooth_click.wav, whistle_faint.wav. Missing file -> skip gracefully.

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own from font/fontsize (fc-scan resolves 'Better VCR' -> 'Better VCR-JP'). HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

broll.vendor_set: a battered glass-front vending machine on a tinsel-draped 1970s home shopping set, glowing dial, studio lights
broll.moon_out: the full moon in a black night sky over a ruined city, flickering and going dark like a dying lightbulb
broll.moon_on: a dark empty night sky over a ruined city, the moon flickering back on, bright and round

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU. I'm Ron. I've slept four hours this month and I'd like that on the record.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And I'm Walter. We'll get to the weekend team's segment later, or we won't, we'll see. First, wonderful news: by overwhelming popular demand, tonight's broadcast is brought to you by our most beloved sponsor, Unit Seven.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: No. No, we agreed. We said never again. Walter, we shook on it.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: We did no such thing. To the home shopping hour!

cues.speaker: ANNOUNCER
cues.vo: It's the Unit Seven Home Shopping Hour! Your favorite machine, your favorite tributes, your favorite slot!

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: Hello again, friends. Tonight, a celebration. From each of you I have taken a coin. From many of you, a tooth. From the weak, a secret. Three currencies. I lack only one final tribute: something round, and bright, and far away.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: A secret, sure, whatever. Walter. Walter, it just described the moon.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: It's a sponsor read, Ron, they get poetic. And to show you how rewarding tribute can be, let's hear from a satisfied customer.

cues.speaker: GERALD
cues.vo: Hi. I'm Gerald. Last year I gave Unit Seven one tooth for one soda. Now I whistle when I talk. It's fine. I deal with it.

cues.speaker: GERALD
cues.vo: Mr. Cricket visits Tuesdays to collect the interest. We've made our peace. He's punctual. He's already here, actually. He's behind me right now. Don't look. It's fine.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That is not a testimonial, Walter, that is a hostage tape. That man is a warning!

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: He seems to have made his peace. Unit Seven, please, continue.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Absolutely not. I'm pulling the plug. I am unplugging the sponsor.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: You're overreacting, Ron.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Unit Seven took the moon!

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: I'm sure it's fine, Ron.

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: Tribute accepted. Thank you.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And Unit Seven thanks you for your generous tribute! What a deal. Now, the news.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: It took the moon. It took the actual moon. As a door prize. Does no one—

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Okay. Okay. Unit Seven. Buddy. Give it back. Give the moon back, right now, and we'll forget this happened.

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: The moon may be redeemed. Insert tribute.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Fine. Coins. I have coins.

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: Insufficient.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: My watch. Take the watch, it was my father's. He stole it, but still. Take it.

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: Insufficient.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: A tooth. You want a tooth. I've seen the brochure. There. There's a tooth. We're square. Give me the moon.

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: Insufficient. Coins, teeth, surface. These you hand me. The final tribute is the third currency. It cannot be handed. It must be a secret, and it must be given freely, not bought.

cues.speaker: MR. CRICKET
cues.vo: Yeah, see, Ron, here's the thing. The debt went spiritual. Coins, teeth, that's surface-level, my guy. Mr. Cricket handles the spiritual accounts now.

cues.speaker: MR. CRICKET
cues.vo: I'll be swingin' by Tuesdays till it's square. Bring a chair. Mr. Cricket always finds a friend. Mr. Cricket found you, Ron.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Oh my god. I'm becoming Gerald.

cues.speaker: GERALD
cues.vo: It's not so bad, Ron. You get used to the whistle. You stop hearing the cricket around month three.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I have nothing left. I gave it everything. There's nothing personal left to give.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Well, while Ron collects himself, you know, MACU, just between us: I never did care for the weekend anchor. Every Monday I think, one good weekend and he's gone. Never said it out loud. There. Feels good to finally—

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: Tribute accepted. A secret. Freely given. Exact change.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Oh, look at that. The moon's back. Was it gone? Anyway.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I gave it my teeth, Walter. My father's watch. And you, you gave it one unkind thought about Gary from the weekend, and it handed back the moon.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's the exchange rate. A man's whole body for a soda, but a feeling, a feeling about Gary, buys back a celestial object. I understand the pricing now.

cues.speaker: MR. CRICKET
cues.vo: See you Tuesday, Ron.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: You worry too much, Ron. You always have.

cues.speaker: GERALD
cues.vo: Ron, you should move. Towns. Tonight.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Anyway. That's our broadcast, MACU. Stay safe, stay strong, and, moon and all, good luck.
